summary: Refreshed the MN rules, name-entry, item-switch, and stage-switch menu split against a current baseline, touching seven src/melee/mn files. The slice improved MN code/data matching, with review attention on pragma codegen tactics in mnruleplus.c and mnstagesw.c and on reverting generated named string literals in mnnamenew.c.

key_files.role: Largest changed file; refreshed rules-menu code/data and added or materialized menu data tables and sdata values.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: mnmainrule.c adds mn_803EC600, mn_803EC7DC, mn_803EC7EC, mn_803EC808, mn_803EC818, mn_804D4B88/lbl_804D4B8C/mn_804D4B90/mn_804D4B94 and rewrites fn_8022F538
text_corpus bot report: main/melee/mn/mnmainrule .data 5.70% to 100.00%; mn_80231804 and fn_802309F0 new matches

key_files.role: Name-entry menu refresh; review flagged generated named string literals that should be reverted to inline literals.
evidence_refs: review_comments: ribbanya comment discussion_r3440019152, 'Revert these named literals.'
review hunk: mnNameNew_EnterFromMnCharSel replaces asset string literals such as "MenMainBack_Top_joint" with mnNameNew_803EE38C-style symbols

key_files.role: Stage-switch menu refresh; includes local variable cleanup and retained inline_depth pragmas for MWCC codegen preservation.
evidence_refs: review_comments: fjooord comment discussion_r3429099048, 'possibly remove these pragmas'

review_comments: fjooord follow-up discussion_r3429270939, removing inline_depth pragmas still compiles but changes MWCC output; kept to preserve codegen; full PR validates with main.dol OK and dol_diff clean

key_files.role: Item-switch menu refresh; loop, switch, local-lifetime, direct table access, and naming changes for matching.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: fn_80233E10 changes item_order indexed loops to pointer-increment loops and if/default logic to switch forms
diff_excerpt: mnItemSw_802351A0 renames data to user_data and updates user_data field uses

key_files.role: Rule-plus menu refresh; review flagged dont_inline pragmas for possible repair.
evidence_refs: review_comments: fjooord comment discussion_r3429094189, 'possibly fix these pragmas'
review hunk: added #pragma dont_inline on near fn_8023201C / mn_80232458

key_files.role: Name menu refresh with matching improvements reported by bot.
evidence_refs: text_corpus bot report: mnName_8023AC40 and mnName_MainInput improved

what_changed: Refreshed the MN rules, name-entry, item-switch, and stage-switch menu split on a current baseline, per PR body.
Changed seven menu translation units under src/melee/mn: mnitemsw.c, mnmainrule.c, mnname.c, mnnamenew.c, mnruleplus.c, mnstagesel.c, and mnstagesw.c.
Added or moved MN rules-menu data in mnmainrule.c, including byte arrays, StaticModelDesc pointer tables, u16 arrays, struct arrays, and sdata float/u8 values.

Reworked several input/menu handlers using switch forms, pointer-increment loops over item_order, local scope adjustments, and clearer local names such as user_data, hover_anim_jobj, cursor_jobj, item_anim, and y_spacing.
Retained some pragma-based codegen controls in mnstagesw.c after local testing showed removing inline_depth pragmas changed MWCC output.

The PR body states ninja changes_all completed successfully and reported only MN fuzzy/matched-code/matched-data improvements; the bot report recorded matched code +2724 bytes and matched data +3072 bytes.

smart_moves.text: When a pragma was suspected as removable, the author tested removal and documented the result: compilation still worked, but MWCC output changed, so the pragma was kept for codegen preservation.
The PR preserved verification context in the body: rebase point, applying the slice alone to a production baseline worktree, running ninja changes_all, and reporting the scope of improvements.
Several generated-looking temporaries were renamed to local role names where the role was clear, such as hover_anim_jobj, cursor_jobj, item_anim, and user_data.

decomp_lessons.lesson: Pragma cleanup should be tested rather than assumed: in this slice, removing inline_depth pragmas still compiled but changed MWCC output, so the author retained them with explicit codegen evidence.

Asset/resource string literals in code-matching PRs should not be replaced with address-style generated symbols without explicit data-ownership evidence; review requested reverting mnNameNew_* named literals back to strings.
evidence_refs: review hunk: mnNameNew_EnterFromMnCharSel string literals replaced by mnNameNew_803EE38C-style symbols

decomp_lessons.lesson: Local variable naming can improve reviewability when it describes a proven local role, but the PR evidence supports only local-role names, not broader semantic renames.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: mnStageSw_80236548 renames sp44 to hover_anim_jobj and data->x28 use to cursor_jobj
diff_excerpt: mnItemSw_802351A0 renames data to user_data

decomp_lessons.lesson: Pointer-increment loops over small data tables can be a matching tactic when the accessed base pointer is kept explicit, as shown by item_order loop rewrites in mnitemsw.c.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: fn_80233E10 changes item_order[i] loops to order pointer increments
diff_excerpt: fn_80234C24 uses u8* order and increments it in the loop

naming_conventions.convention: Use short local role names for HSD_JObj pointers when the role is clear, such as hover_anim_jobj for hover animation jobjs and cursor_jobj for cursor jobjs.
Use user_data for allocated or recovered GObj user-data storage when distinguishing it from other Mn*Data pointers in the same function.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: mnItemSw_802351A0 renames MnItemSwData* data to user_data and uses it for GObj_InitUserData

naming_conventions.convention: Do not convert descriptive asset string literals into address-style mnNameNew_803EExxx symbols unless the PR is explicitly doing data ownership work and evidence supports the symbol.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Retain narrowly scoped inline_depth pragmas only after testing removal and confirming MWCC output changes.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.risk: Pragmas are exceptional codegen steering and should remain evidence-backed rather than style.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Use switch/default shapes in menu input paths where they improve matching over if/else forms.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: mnitemsw.c fn_80233E10 changes confirmed_selection and gm_801A4310 branches to switch forms
diff_excerpt: mnmainrule.c fn_8022F538 changes gm_801A4310 branch to switch/default
assembly_or_matching_tactics.risk: The slice does not prove switch is a global convention; treat as local matching evidence.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Adjust local declaration order and lifetimes, including moving temporaries into inner blocks, to match MWCC stack/register behavior.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: mnitemsw.c moves y_spacing/anim_val into arg1 block and sp3C into arg2 block
review hunk: mnstagesw.c introduces inner HSD_JObj* sp3C
assembly_or_matching_tactics.risk: Local lifetime steering should be kept narrow and reviewed under matching-tactics evidence rules.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Materialize or relocate data objects when bot/report evidence shows matched-data improvements and the PR scope includes data matching.
evidence_refs: text_corpus bot report: mnmainrule .data reaches 100%; matched data +3072 bytes
assembly_or_matching_tactics.risk: Do not generalize this to naming ordinary string literals; review separately rejected named string literals in mnnamenew.c.

review_feedback.author: fjooord
review_feedback.comment: I tested removing these inline_depth pragmas locally; it still compiles, but MWCC output changes, so I kept them to preserve codegen. The full PR validates with main.dol OK and dol_diff clean.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs touching mnnamenew.c after review comment 'Revert these named literals' to confirm whether mnNameNew_803EE38C-style string symbols were reverted.
Search for retained #pragma dont_inline or inline_depth blocks in src/melee/mn/mnruleplus.c and src/melee/mn/mnstagesw.c with nearby comments documenting MWCC output evidence.
Search MN menu TUs for pointer-increment item_order loops to compare local authored/matching style.
Search for MenMainBack_Top_joint and related mnNameNew_EnterFromMnCharSel asset strings to verify current literal-vs-symbol state.
Search bot reports around PR2726 for mnmainrule .data 100% and mnstagesw .sdata 100% to correlate data materialization with final merged source.

evidence_refs: changed_files.jsonl: seven changed files and hunk counts
review_comments: four review comments, one truncated loaded file
text_corpus: bot report excerpt with code/data improvements
notes: The loaded diff and review_comments files are truncated, so file-level details are representative rather than exhaustive.
The PR is marked merged in metadata, but this intake does not independently validate final source state after review comments.
The PR body reports ninja changes_all success; a review comment reports main.dol OK and dol_diff clean for the full PR. These are accepted as PR evidence, not rerun validation.
Review comments include one clear reviewer request from ribbanya about named literals and author self-comments about pragmas; do not infer additional reviewer intent beyond the written comments.

accepted_candidate_records.title: PR2726 retained inline_depth pragmas only after MWCC-output testing
accepted_candidate_records.text: In PR2726, mnstagesw.c had pragmas questioned for removal. The author reported testing removal locally: it still compiled, but MWCC output changed, so the pragmas were kept to preserve codegen; the full PR validated with main.dol OK and dol_diff clean.

accepted_candidate_records.title: PR2726 review rejected generated named string literals in mnnamenew.c
accepted_candidate_records.text: A review comment on mnNameNew_EnterFromMnCharSel requested reverting named literals where inline asset/resource strings such as MenMainBack_Top_joint had been replaced by mnNameNew_803EExxx symbols.

source_update_candidates.title: Add PR2726 as an example for no string-literal symbol regression
source_update_candidates.text: Use PR2726 mnnamenew.c review as an example: replacing asset/resource strings in lbArchive_LoadSections-style calls with address-style mnNameNew_803EExxx symbols prompted reviewer feedback to revert named literals.
source_update_candidates.reason: This is well aligned with existing standards and provides a concrete historical review example, but should be curated rather than promoted by intake.

source_update_candidates.title: Add PR2726 as an example for evidence-backed retained pragmas
source_update_candidates.text: Use PR2726 mnstagesw.c as an example where pragma removal was tested; compilation succeeded but MWCC output changed, so inline_depth pragmas were retained with explicit validation context.
source_update_candidates.reason: The PR provides a concise example of the required evidence boundary for keeping exceptional codegen tactics.

source_update_candidates.title: MN menu split has historical pointer-loop and switch-shape matching examples in PR2726
source_update_candidates.text: PR2726 shows mnitemsw.c and mnmainrule.c matching work using pointer-increment loops over menu data and switch/default forms in menu input handlers. Treat as path-local historical evidence, not a global rule.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: fn_80233E10 item_order loops and switch forms
source_update_candidates.reason: May help future MN menu decomp work search local historical tactics, but the loaded slice is truncated and should be verified before adding a path fact.

rejection_notes: Do not promote 'use pragmas for MN matching' as a standard; the evidence only supports retaining specific pragmas after removal changed MWCC output.
Do not promote address-style mnNameNew_803EExxx symbols for asset strings; the only review evidence asks to revert them.
Do not infer that all switch rewrites or pointer-increment loops are globally preferred; the evidence is local to this PR's matching changes.
Do not claim final post-review source state for the named literals or pragmas from this slice alone, because the loaded files are truncated and intake did not inspect the merged tree.
